content_block.content: **Flights:** Fly from Hong Kong (HKG) or Shenzhen (SZX) directly to Da Nang (DAD). Return from Da Nang or from Ho Chi Minh City (SGN) if extending to Saigon.
Day 1: Arrival in Da Nang & Local Immersion (4 Apr)

• **Afternoon:** Arrive at Da Nang International Airport (DAD), transfer to accommodation.
• **Local markets:** Visit Han Market or Con Market for fresh produce, local specialties, handicrafts.
• **Evening:** Explore Helio Night Market (food, entertainment, shopping).
Day 2: Da Nang’s Cultural & Craft Highlights (5 Apr)

• **Morning:** Marble Mountains – explore caves, tunnels, Buddhist temples (Thuy Son).
• **Nearby:** Non Nuoc Stone Carving Village – watch artisans craft marble sculptures.
• **Afternoon:** Museum of Cham Sculpture – 5th‑15th century Cham artifacts.
• **Evening:** Stroll along Han River, see Dragon Bridge fire/water show (weekends).
Day 3: Travel to Hue & Imperial Grandeur (6 Apr)

• **Morning:** Scenic train/bus/private car from Da Nang to Hue via Hai Van Pass (stop at Lap An Lagoon, Lang Co Beach).
• **Afternoon:** Hue Imperial City (UNESCO) – guided walk through Forbidden Purple City, Ngo Mon Gate, Thai Hoa Palace.
• **Evening:** Boat tour on Perfume River, listen to traditional Hue singing.
Day 4: Hue’s Royal Tombs & Traditional Crafts (7 Apr)

• **Morning:** Nguyen Dynasty Royal Tombs along Perfume River – Minh Mang, Tu Duc (unique architecture, serene landscapes).
• **Afternoon:** Craft villages – Thuy Xuan Incense Village (make your own incense), lotus‑leaf conical‑hat workshops, rice‑paper making.
• **Evening:** Panoramic view from Vong Canh Hill.
Day 5: Travel to Hoi An & Ancient Town Charm (8 Apr)

• **Morning:** Bus transfer from Hue to Hoi An (3‑4 hours) with stops at Hai Van Pass, Lap An Lagoon, Thanh Toan Tiles‑Roofed Bridge, Marble Mountains.
• **Afternoon:** Hoi An Ancient Town walking tour – Japanese Covered Bridge, Phuc Kien Assembly Hall, Tan Ky Old House, Historic & Cultural Museum.
• **Evening:** Lantern‑making workshop, dinner in lantern‑lit streets.
Day 6: Hoi An’s Handicrafts & Countryside Life (9 Apr)

• **Morning:** Thanh Ha Pottery Village (500‑year‑old) – try pottery making.
• **Late morning:** Tra Que Vegetable Village – organic farming activities, cooking class with fresh herbs.
• **Afternoon:** Kim Bong Carpentry Village (wood carving) or leather‑craft workshop.
• **Evening:** Free time for shopping, revisit favorite spots.
Day 7: Hoi An Free Time & Departure / Extension to Saigon (10 Apr)

• **Morning:** Leisurely morning – revisit favorite spot, last‑minute souvenir shopping.
• **Optional basket‑boat ride:** Coconut forest in Cam Thanh village, crab‑catching with local fishermen.
• **Afternoon:** Transfer to Da Nang Airport (DAD) for departure **or** fly to Saigon (Ho Chi Minh City) for extension.

• **Morning:** Fly from Da Nang to Ho Chi Minh City (SGN).
• **Afternoon:** Explore Saigon’s highlights – Notre‑Dame Cathedral, Central Post Office, War Remnants Museum, Ben Thanh Market.
• **Evening:** Depart from SGN back to HK/SZ.

• **Culture & history:** Covered in Hue Imperial City, royal tombs, Cham Museum, Hoi An Ancient Town.
• **Handicrafts & local markets:** Non Nuoc stone carving, Hue incense/conical‑hat villages, Hoi An pottery/carpentry/leather workshops, Han/Con markets.
• **Natural exploration:** Marble Mountains, Hai Van Pass, Lap An Lagoon, Perfume River, coconut‑forest basket boats.
• **Local life experiences:** Market visits, farming activities (Tra Que), lantern‑making, boat tours, night markets.
